summary: Tests 1/5/6/7 are truthful RED-genuine (proven by source mutation: A neuters NOT NULL -> 1/5/7 RED; B alters backfill value -> test 6 RED). No tautologies/false-passes. 1 WARN: backfill whereNull selectivity unpinned (mutation C clobbers all rows, 7/7 still pass).

findings.claim: Backfill is fully covered (TEST-CATALOG branch-enumeration claims the whereNull backfill is pinned by test 6)
findings.evidence: Mutation C removed `.whereNull('timezone')` so up() overwrites EVERY users row to 'America/New_York' -> all 7 tests still PASS. No test seeds an existing non-null timezone (e.g. 'Europe/London') to prove the backfill leaves it untouched; a regression that clobbers real user timezones would false-pass.
findings.fix: Add a test: insert one user timezone='Europe/London' + one timezone=NULL, run down->insert->up, assert London preserved and NULL backfilled to 'America/New_York'.

findings.evidence: Tests 2,3,4 pass in both pre- and post-migration states (DEFAULT + COLLATION already correct in initial schema) so cannot go RED pre-migration; preservation guards only. Telly disclosed this honestly.

findings.claim: Reversibility test 7 fully verifies down() state
findings.evidence: Down phase asserts only IS_NULLABLE; DEFAULT + COLLATION are not re-asserted after rollback, so a down() that dropped them would not be caught (low risk - down() ALTER is explicit).
findings.fix: Optional: assert COLUMN_DEFAULT + COLLATION_NAME in the down state.

refers.why: W1 coverage gap (preserve-existing-timezone backfill test) is telly's to author
